overview.description: Vision AI platform for physical product authentication, fingerprinting, and digital product passports
overview.description_long: Veracity Protocol builds a protocol of trust for physical objects, using proprietary Vision AI models to create unique microstructure fingerprints that enable non-invasive serialization, authentication, classification, traceability, and secure digital product passports. Their technology works without adding physical tags, QR codes, RFID chips or holograms — instead leveraging the natural microstructure of the product itself. They serve industries including pharma, packaging, automotive, aerospace, agrochemicals, luxury goods, and collectibles.
overview.tagline: The Standard of Trust for Physical Objects

history.origin_story: Founded by three Czech technical co-founders (CEO, CTO, COO) focused on building Vision AI-based product authentication to fight counterfeiting and enable trust for physical goods in the digital world. Went through Techstars accelerator in 2019.

key_milestones.event: Named Top 10 AI-Blockchain Startup by Techstars
Named Top 20 Vision IoT Startup by Plug & Play
Named Top 10 Vision AI Startup by Starburst
Member of Cirpass2 EWG and Community of Practice under EC Digital Europe program (Digital Product Passport)

awards: Top 20 Vision IoT Startup — Plug & Play 2020
Member, Cirpass2 EWG — EC Digital Europe 2024
Member, Community of Practice — EC Digital Europe 2024

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Veracity Protocol's core IP lies in proprietary Vision AI models trained specifically on physical object microstructures to generate tamper-proof fingerprints. This is a hardware+AI combination requiring calibrated physical imaging; generic foundation models cannot replicate the non-invasive physical fingerprinting capability without the specialized training data and domain expertise in materials science.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Real and growing market driven by EU Digital Product Passport regulation (Cirpass2 membership shows regulatory alignment), but the anti-counterfeiting and product authentication market has incumbent players (RFID/NFC providers, hologram vendors). Their non-invasive approach is differentiating but requires changing established industry workflows.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No publicly documented funding rounds beyond early Techstars participation. Small team (~30-50 employees) with no visible VC backing or public revenue signals. Reliance on B2B enterprise sales in regulated sectors can result in long sales cycles with uncertain runway.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Proprietary Vision AI tech moat and strong EU regulatory tailwinds (Digital Product Passports) provide differentiation and market pull. However, undisclosed funding and early-stage enterprise sales challenges elevate risk above companies with established revenue or VC backing.

metadata.notes: No CNAIP URL available. Careers page returns 404. Archived company page from 2023 (Wayback Machine) provided team/founder names. Founded year estimated at 2019 based on Techstars award. Team size in 2023 was 30-40 (23+ developers named plus leadership); used 11-50 range as conservative estimate.
